attributes.description: Alerts when `postgresql.temp_files > 0`. Queries spilling to disk indicates work_mem pressure.
attributes.name: [PostgreSQL OTel] Temp files created

investigation_guide.blob:
## PostgreSQL temp files created

### What fired
`postgresql.temp_files` increased during the window.

### Why it matters
Any temp file creation means a query exceeded `work_mem` and spilled to disk. Frequent spilling is a strong tuning signal.

### Triage
1. Identify the queries causing temp files.

### Remediation
- Raise `work_mem`.
- Rewrite queries to reduce memory.

### Tuning
- Fires on any creation.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-postgresqlreceiver.otel-*
// Temp file creation indicates queries spilling to disk (insufficient work_mem or poor query plans)
| WHERE postgresql.temp_files IS NOT NULL
| EVAL temp_files_val = TO_DOUBLE(postgresql.temp_files)
| WHERE temp_files_val > 0
| STATS temp_file_count = SUM(temp_files_val) BY service.instance.id, postgresql.database.name
| WHERE temp_file_count > 0
